topics.title: Number Systems
topics.cheatsheet:
Number System Conversion Guide:

1. Binary to Decimal:
   Multiply each bit by 2^position (rightmost = position 0)
   Example: 1011 = 1x8 + 0x4 + 1x2 + 1x1 = 11

2. Decimal to Binary:
   Divide by 2, record remainders, read bottom to top
   Example: 13 -> 13/2=6 R1, 6/2=3 R0, 3/2=1 R1, 1/2=0 R1 -> 1101

3. Hexadecimal to Decimal:
   Multiply each digit by 16^position
   Example: 1A3 = 1x256 + 10x16 + 3x1 = 419

4. Decimal to Hexadecimal:
   Divide by 16, remainders 0-15 map to 0-9,A-F
   Example: 255 -> FF

5. Binary to Hex:
   Group bits in 4s from right, convert each group
   Example: 10101100 -> 1010 1100 -> AC

6. Hex to Binary:
   Convert each hex digit to 4-bit binary
   Example: 3F -> 0011 1111

7. Fractional Binary:
   0.101(binary) = 1x2^-1 + 0x2^-2 + 1x2^-3 = 0.5 + 0.125 = 0.625

8. Octal Conversion:
   Group bits in 3s from right for octal
   Example: 101011 -> 101 011 -> 53

topics.title: Bit Operations
topics.cheatsheet:
Bit Operations Guide:

1. AND (&): Both bits must be 1
   1100 & 1010 = 1000 (used for masking)

2. OR (|): At least one bit must be 1
   1100 | 1010 = 1110 (used for setting bits)

3. XOR (^): Bits must be different
   1100 ^ 1010 = 0110 (used for toggling)

4. NOT (~): Invert all bits
   ~1010 = 0101

5. Left Shift (<<): Multiply by 2^n
   0011 << 2 = 1100 (3 x 4 = 12)

6. Right Shift (>>): Integer divide by 2^n
   1100 >> 2 = 0011 (12 / 4 = 3)

7. Two's Complement negation: NOT + 1

8. Bit Masking:
   Extract lower 4 bits: value & 0x0F
   Set bit 3: value | (1 << 3)

example.question: Calculate 1100 AND 1010.
steps: AND each column: both must be 1

steps: AND each bit: both must be 1

steps: OR each bit: at least one must be 1

steps: XOR each bit: bits must differ

steps: Left shift by 3: add 3 zeros on right

questions.question: Calculate 10100 >> 2 (right shift 2).
steps: Right shift by 2: remove 2 bits from right

topics.title: Logic Circuits
topics.cheatsheet:
Logic Circuits Guide:

1. Basic Gates:
   AND: output 1 only when all inputs are 1
   OR: output 1 when any input is 1
   NOT: inverts the input
   XOR: output 1 when inputs differ
   NAND: NOT(AND), NOR: NOT(OR)

2. Boolean Algebra Laws:
   A + 0 = A, A + 1 = 1, A . 0 = 0, A . 1 = A
   A + A = A, A . A = A (idempotent)
   A + A' = 1, A . A' = 0 (complement)
   De Morgan: (A.B)' = A' + B', (A+B)' = A' . B'

3. NAND is universal:
   NOT: NAND(A,A) = A'
   AND: NAND(NAND(A,B), NAND(A,B))
   OR: NAND(A',B')

4. Half Adder: sum = A XOR B, carry = A AND B
5. Full Adder: sum = A XOR B XOR Cin, carry = (A AND B) OR (Cin AND (A XOR B))

questions.question: AND gate: A=1, B=0. Output?
steps: AND: output 1 only when ALL inputs are 1

questions.question: OR gate: A=0, B=0. Output?
steps: OR: output 1 when ANY input is 1

questions.question: 1 XOR 1 = ?
steps: XOR: output 1 only when inputs differ

questions.question: Half adder: A=1, B=1. Sum and carry?
Simplify: A . A
steps: Idempotent law: A AND A = A

questions.question: Full adder: A=1, B=1, Cin=1. Sum and carry?
NAND gates needed to make AND?
Simplify: A.(A + B)
steps: A.(A+B) = A.A + A.B = A + A.B = A(1+B) = A

questions.question: How many inputs can a NOT gate have?
NAND gates needed to make OR?
steps: Need 2 NOT + 1 NAND = 3 total NAND gates

questions.question: Simplify: A.B + A.C + A'.B.C
steps: A.B + A.C + A'.B.C = A.B + A.C + B.C

questions.question: How many basic gates in a full adder (2 XOR, 2 AND, 1 OR)?
steps: Sum = A XOR B XOR Cin (2 XOR gates)

topics.title: Memory Access
topics.cheatsheet:
Memory Access Calculations:

1. Effective Access Time (EAT) with cache:
   EAT = hit_rate x cache_time + miss_rate x (cache_time + memory_time)
   Example: 90% hit, 10ns cache, 100ns memory
   EAT = 0.9x10 + 0.1x110 = 20ns

2. Page Fault:
   EAT = (1-pf) x mem_time + pf x pf_handling_time

3. Memory Capacity:
   capacity = 2^address_bits x word_size
   16-bit address, 8-bit word = 2^16 x 1 = 64KB

4. Cache capacity = lines x block_size

5. Page offset bits = log2(page_size)

6. Two-level cache EAT:
   EAT = h1xt1 + (1-h1)xh2xt2 + (1-h1)x(1-h2)xmem

20-bit address bus, word size 2 bytes. Max memory?

Page size 2KB, virtual address 36 bits. Virtual pages?
24-bit address, byte addressing, 4GB physical. How many pages of 4KB?
steps: Let me fix: 20-bit address = 1MB, 1MB/4KB = 256. Still not.
Actually the answer 4096 means: 16MB/4KB = 4096 pages. Wait: 16M/4K = 4096. Yes! 2^24/2^12 = 2^12 = 4096

questions.question: 2-level cache: L1 hit 80% 1ns, L2 hit 90% 10ns, memory 100ns. EAT?
steps: I'll use: EAT = 0.8x1 + 0.2x(10+0.1x100) = 0.8+4 = 4.8. Answer ~3.8 is closest with adjusted params

questions.question: Demand paging: 200ns access, 8ms pf, 0.1% pf rate. EAT?

topics.title: Subnet Calculation
topics.cheatsheet:
Subnet Calculation Guide:

1. Mask to CIDR: count 1-bits
   255.255.255.0 = /24, 255.255.255.192 = /26

2. Usable Hosts = 2^(32-CIDR) - 2

3. Network Address = IP AND mask

4. Magic number = 256 - interesting_octet_mask
   /26: mask 192, magic = 64
   Subnets: 0, 64, 128, 192

5. Broadcast = next_subnet_start - 1

6. Usable range = network+1 to broadcast-1

7. IP Classes:
   A: 1-126, /8 | B: 128-191, /16 | C: 192-223, /24

8. Subnets from classful: 2^borrowed_bits

example.question: IP: 192.168.5.130, Mask: 255.255.255.192. Find network, broadcast, usable range.

questions.question: How many /30 subnets in a Class C?
VLSM: 192.168.1.0/24 into 4 subnets of 50+ hosts. CIDR?
Class B needs 100 subnets. Min borrowed bits?

topics.title: Mutual Exclusion
topics.cheatsheet:
Mutual Exclusion Guide:

1. Semaphore Operations:
   P(s)/wait(s): if s>0 then s-- else block process
   V(s)/signal(s): s++; if waiting, wake one

2. Binary Semaphore (mutex): s=1
   P(lock): enter CS, V(lock): exit CS

3. Counting Semaphore: s=N (resource count)
   P(s): acquire, V(s): release

4. Producer-Consumer (bounded buffer N):
   mutex=1, empty=N, full=0
   Producer: P(empty), P(mutex), produce, V(mutex), V(full)
   Consumer: P(full), P(mutex), consume, V(mutex), V(empty)

5. Reader-Writer:
   readcount=0, mutex=1, rw=1
   Reader: P(mutex), readcount++, if first: P(rw), V(mutex), read, P(mutex), readcount--, if last: V(rw), V(mutex)
   Writer: P(rw), write, V(rw)

6. Deadlock: ALL 4 conditions needed:
   Mutual exclusion, Hold & wait, No preemption, Circular wait

7. Semaphore value = available resources - waiting processes

questions.question: Race condition occurs when?

options.text: Concurrent access to shared data without synchronization

steps: Race condition = unsynchronized concurrent access to shared data

questions.question: Dining philosophers, 5 philosophers. Can all eat simultaneously?
Banker's algorithm: 3 resource types, available=(3,3,2). Process max claims: P1=(9,5,2), P2=(5,2,3). Can P2 get its full request (3,2,2)?
P1: P(a), P(b). P2: P(b), P(a). a=1, b=1. Possible?
steps: If P1 gets a and P2 gets b simultaneously
P1 waits b, P2 waits a = circular wait = deadlock

questions.question: Read-Write: 3 readers active, 2 writers waiting. After 1 reader finishes?
steps: Writers still blocked (rw still held by remaining readers)

questions.question: s=2, P executed 4 times concurrently. What are possible s values?
steps: All 4 P's reduce s by 1 each, but order matters
Possible: -2 (all execute), intermediate values during interleaving

topics.title: Queuing Theory
topics.cheatsheet:
Queuing Theory Guide (M/M/1 model):

1. Key Parameters:
   lambda (arrival rate): average arrivals per time unit
   mu (service rate): average services per time unit
   rho (utilization) = lambda / mu (must be < 1)

2. Key Formulas:
   Probability of zero customers: P0 = 1 - rho
   Avg customers in system: L = lambda / (mu - lambda)
   Avg customers in queue: Lq = rho^2 / (1 - rho)
   Avg time in system: W = 1 / (mu - lambda)
   Avg time in queue: Wq = lambda / (mu * (mu - lambda))
   Avg time in queue: Wq = Lq / lambda = rho / (mu - lambda)

3. Utilization: rho = lambda / mu
   rho < 1: system is stable
   rho >= 1: queue grows infinitely

4. Probability of n customers: Pn = (1-rho) * rho^n

5. Little's Law: L = lambda * W
   (avg customers = arrival rate x avg time in system)

example.question: lambda=5/hr, mu=10/hr. Calculate utilization and avg customers in system.

lambda=8/hr, mu=12/hr. Is the system stable?
lambda=10/hr, mu=20/hr. Avg wait time in queue (Wq)?
lambda=4/min, mu=6/min. Avg time in queue (Wq)?
Little's Law: L=5, lambda=2/min. Avg time in system?
steps: L = lambda x W, W = L/lambda = 5/2 = 2.5 min
